input.title: Enterprise Resource Planning System
input.description: This document provides a comprehensive Software Requirements Specification (SRS) for the development of an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) System The system is a web-based platform designed to streamline and automate core business processes, including finance, human resources, procurement, sales, inventory, and customer relationship management (CRM)

user_classes.Admin: Full access to all system features, including user management, data configuration, and system settings.
user_classes.Manager: Controls departmental functionalities, generates reports, and tracks KPIs.
user_classes.Employee: Limited access to system features based on roles such as HR, finance, or sales.

definitions.Admin: A user with full administrative privileges to configure and manage the system.
definitions.Manager: A user responsible for managing specific business units and overseeing day-to-day operations.
definitions.Employee: A user with limited access based on assigned roles, such as HR staff or sales personnel.
definitions.ERP: Enterprise Resource Planning, a suite of integrated applications used to manage core business processes.
definitions.CRM: Customer Relationship Management, a tool for managing customer interactions.
definitions.HRM: Human Resource Management, a system to manage employee data and payroll.

modules: User Management with Role-Based Access Control
scope: support a set of integrated functionalities designed to improve operational efficiency
real-time insights across various business functions
out_of_scope: Mobile applications (iOS/Android) will not be developed in the initial release
Integration with third-party e-commerce platforms or external accounting tools is out of scope for the first version
constraints: No mobile application is part of the initial release
No integration with third-party accounting tools will be included in the first release
The system will support multi-user configurations only
Initially, the system will be in English with the possibility for future localization

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based, accessible via modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari).
operating_environment.backend: Node.js with Express framework, MongoDB for data storage, and RESTful APIs for system communication.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-based infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud).

user_interface: The user interface will be web-based, responsive, and accessible according to WCAG 2.1 standards
hardware_interfaces: No specific hardware interfaces are required, as the system will be deployed in the cloud
software_interfaces: The ERP system will interface with external systems like payment gateways, email servers, and third-party APIs
communication_interfaces: RESTful APIs will be used for communication between the frontend and backend, and the system will support integration with other platforms

FR-01.title: User Authentication
requirements: Users must be able to register, log in, and log out securely
Support authentication via email/password and Single Sign-On (SSO)
Passwords should be hashed and stored securely
Strong password policies (minimum 8 characters, one uppercase letter, and one number) must be enforced

FR-02.title: User Role Management
requirements: Admins can assign different roles to users (Admin, Manager, Employee)
Each role has specific permissions and access levels to different parts of the system
Users can be granted access to specific modules based on their role

FR-03.title: Financial Transaction Tracking
requirements: The system must track revenue, expenses, accounts payable, and accounts receivable
Financial data should be recorded in real-time and stored securely
Admin users should be able to generate financial reports like balance sheets, profit and loss statements, and cash flow statements

FR-04.title: Payroll Management
requirements: The system must calculate and process employee payroll
Payroll data must include salary, bonuses, taxes, and deductions
Payroll reports should be generated for each payroll cycle

FR-05.title: Sales and CRM
requirements: The system must track sales activities, including lead generation, opportunities, and sales conversion
The CRM module must allow users to manage customer interactions, including email, phone calls, and meetings
Managers should be able to track sales performance and generate sales reports

FR-06.title: Inventory Management
requirements: The system must track product inventory, including quantities and locations
Inventory records should be updated in real-time with every sale, purchase, or return
The system must provide low-stock alerts to users

FR-07.title: Procurement Management
requirements: The system must allow users to automate the procurement process
Users must be able to create purchase orders, track supplier relationships, and manage inventory levels
Procurement reports must be generated for analysis

FR-08.title: Time and Attendance Management
requirements: The system must allow employees to log their working hours, including overtime
HR personnel should be able to generate attendance reports based on logged hours
Time-off requests and approvals must be handled by the system

FR-09.title: Performance Evaluation
requirements: The system must track employee performance metrics and generate performance evaluation reports
Managers should be able to set goals and objectives for each employee and track progress over time

FR-10.title: Real-time Reporting and Dashboards
requirements: The system must provide dashboards that display key performance indicators (KPIs)
Reports should be generated in real-time for departments such as finance, sales, inventory, and HR
Managers and admins should have access to performance data for decision-making

FR-11.title: Document Management
requirements: The system should allow users to upload and store business documents such as contracts, invoices, and receipts
Documents should be organized by department and user access levels

FR-12.title: Customer Order Management
requirements: The system must allow employees to manage customer orders, including order creation, fulfillment, and delivery tracking
Admins should be able to generate order history reports

FR-13.title: Supplier Management
requirements: The system must track supplier information, including contact details, pricing, and delivery schedules
Admins should be able to generate supplier performance reports

FR-14.title: Vendor Payment Management
requirements: The system must manage vendor payments and track payment history
Accounts payable personnel should be able to generate vendor payment reports

FR-15.title: Bank Reconciliation
requirements: The system must allow users to reconcile bank transactions with the financial data stored in the ERP
Bank transaction history should be imported automatically for comparison

FR-16.title: Project Management
requirements: The system must allow users to create, assign, and track projects
Managers should be able to set deadlines, track milestones, and generate project reports

FR-17.title: Asset Management
requirements: The system should allow for the management and tracking of company assets such as machinery, vehicles, and office equipment
Asset depreciation should be automatically calculated and tracked

FR-18.title: Audit Trail and Log Management
requirements: The system should maintain an audit trail for all actions taken by users
Logs should include data access, data changes, and system modifications
Admins should have the ability to view and export log data

FR-19.title: Multi-Currency Support
requirements: The system should support multi-currency transactions, including exchange rate calculations
Users should be able to generate financial reports in multiple currencies

FR-20.title: Tax Management
requirements: The system must allow for the configuration of tax rates and rules based on geographical location
The system must automatically calculate taxes for financial transactions

FR-21.title: Marketing Campaign Tracking
requirements: The CRM module must track marketing campaigns, including campaign budgets, channels, and performance
Managers should be able to generate campaign performance reports

FR-22.title: Data Backup and Restore
requirements: The system must perform regular automated backups of data
Users should be able to restore data from backups if needed

FR-23.title: System Notifications and Alerts
requirements: The system must send notifications for important events such as low stock, pending approvals, or upcoming deadlines
Users should be able to configure notification preferences

FR-24.title: Workflow Automation
requirements: The system must automate common workflows such as approval processes, purchase requisitions, and payroll processing
Custom workflows should be configurable by admins

FR-25.title: User Activity Monitoring
requirements: The system should allow admins to monitor user activity within the system
Activity logs should include login times, actions taken, and system access

FR-26.title: Helpdesk and Support Ticketing
requirements: The system should include a helpdesk module for tracking support tickets
Users should be able to create, assign, and track the status of support tickets

FR-27.title: Role-Based Access Control
requirements: The system should implement role-based access control (RBAC) to restrict access to specific functionalities
Users should only have access to the modules relevant to their roles

FR-28.title: User Self-Service Portal
requirements: The system must allow employees to manage their personal information, time-off requests, and payroll records via a self-service portal

FR-29.title: Data Encryption
requirements: All sensitive data, including financial information, employee records, and customer details, must be encrypted both in transit and at rest

FR-30.title: System Availability and Downtime
requirements: The system should be available 99.95% of the time, with downtime scheduled only during maintenance periods

system_attributes.reliability: The system should be available 99.95% of the time, excluding planned maintenance
system_attributes.scalability: The system should support horizontal scaling to accommodate growing data and user traffic
system_attributes.security: The system must support SSL encryption and strong user authentication mechanisms
system_attributes.maintainability: The system should be modular and easy to maintain with robust logging and error handling

FR-01.title: Plan User Authentication Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for user authentication and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Users must be able to register, log in, and log out securely; Support authentication via email/password and Single Sign-On (SSO); Passwords should be hashed and stored securely.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for user authentication is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for user authentication are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for user authentication can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement User Authentication Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for user authentication on React, Node.js, MongoDB, Express. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Users must be able to register, log in, and log out securely; Support authentication via email/password and Single Sign-On (SSO); Passwords should be hashed and stored securely.
acceptance_criteria: Core user authentication business rules are implemented for Enterprise Resource Planning System
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for user authentication
API/service behavior for user authentication is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement User Authentication Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for user authentication with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The user authentication user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for user authentication behave correctly
The user authentication UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for User Authentication
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by user authentication, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by user authentication are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for user authentication integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for user authentication are handled securely

FR-01.title: Harden User Authentication Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to user authentication. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for user authentication are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in user authentication are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for user authentication passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write User Authentication Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for user authentication. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for user authentication are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for user authentication are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical user authentication scenarios
